History
Arcane Acceleration was first introduced in the Trial mission The Law of Retribution, which was retired in Update 22.14 (2018-03-01).
The Operation: Scarlet Spear event sold Arcane Acceleration from Little Duck for 1,500 ô Scarletô Credits.
The Operation: Orphix Venom event sold Arcane Acceleration from Father for 300 ô Phasicô Cells.
The Operation: Gargoyle's Cry event sold Arcane Acceleration from the Vigile Jahu Gargoyle for 5 ô Grotesqueô Splinter.
The Operation: Belly of the Beast event sold Arcane Acceleration from Ordis for 3 ô Volatileô Motes in the first run of the event, and 4 Motes in the second, with a purchase limit of 42.
The Operation: Eight Claw event sold Arcane Acceleration from Dominus Thrax in bundles of 7, costing 2 ô Dominusô Aureus, with a purchase limit of 3 (21 Arcanes in total).
Notes
Additively stacks with mods that increase fire rate (e.g. ô Speedô Trigger).
Does not double its bonus for bows
Bonus applies to most primary weapons, including snipers & launchers, but not shotguns.
Can be triggered by critical hits from almost any source, including all weapon types, beast and sentinel attacks, and even Warframe abilities (such as ô Wisp's ô Breachô Surge sparks and all of ô Gyre's damaging abilities)
Patch History

Update 31.6 (2022-06-09)

ARCANE STAT CHANGES PER RANK
Weãve adjusted the values of older Arcanes with the goal of making lower rank Arcanes more viable, and more desirable while collecting as a result. In short, there are three stats that each Arcane tends to have (speaking generally) -- for example, a percentage chance, a stat buff, and the duration of the buff.
Certain Arcanes increase all of these stats per rank (making them scale exponentially), but we are changing it so that only one stat is affected by rank (making it scale linearly). The other stats will now be at the max-rank value regardless of the Arcane Rank, thereby buffing lower-rank Arcanes as a result.

Arcane Acceleration
On Critical Hit: 30% (was 5%) chance for 15%/90% Fire Rate to Primary Weapons (excluding Shotguns) for 9s (was 2s)
Update 22.12 (2018-02-09)

Now has a base effect of 15% with a base chance of 5%.
Update 16.0 (2015-03-19)

Introduced.

History
Arcane Aegis was first introduced in the Trial mission The Jordas Verdict, which was retired in Update 22.14 (2018-03-01).
The Operation: Scarlet Spear event sold Arcane Aegis from Little Duck for 2,000 ô Scarletô Credits.
The Operation: Orphix Venom event sold Arcane Aegis from Father for 400 ô Phasicô Cells.
The Operation: Gargoyle's Cry event sold Arcane Aegis from the Vigile Jahu Gargoyle for 6 ô Grotesqueô Splinter.
The Operation: Belly of the Beast event sold Arcane Aegis from Ordis for 5 ô Volatileô Motes in the first run of the event, and 6 Motes in the second, with a purchase limit of 42.
The Operation: Eight Claw event sold Arcane Aegis from Dominus Thrax in bundles of 7, costing 4 ô Dominusô Aureus, with a purchase limit of 3 (21 Arcanes total).
The Operation: Blood of Perita event sold Arcane Aegis from Roathe for 4 ô Marksô ofô Valiance with a purchase limit of 21 per week.
Notes
Has a hidden stat that sets shield recharge delay to 0 seconds while active. This causes the players shields to constantly regenerate, even when being damaged.
ô Magnetic status effect still prevents shield regeneration with this Arcane active.
Shield Recharge bonus is additive to the innate 5% shield recharge rate.
Shield recharge bonus stacks multiplicatively with ô Fastô Deflection and ô Vigilanteô Vigor.
A ô Hildryn with ô Redirection, ô Fastô Deflection, and ô Arcaneô Aegis equipped will have a shield recharge rate of
[ 15 + ( 5% + ô 30% ) * 1780 * ( 1 + ô 100% ) ] * (1 + ô 90%) =ô 2,395.9 shields per second while ô Arcaneô Aegis is active.
Does not generate overshields.
Since this Arcane only triggers when shields are damaged, it has no benefit on ô Inaros, ô Kullervo, and ô Nidus, as they have no shields to begin with, or during a Nightmare Mode mission with the No Shields modifier.
Bugs
Activating Arcane Aegis once will break the 80% Shield Recharge cap for the rest of the mission or until death.
After the cap is lifted, ô Gauss's Passive, ô Jade's ô Symphonyô ofô Mercy, and ô Vazarin ability ô Guardianô Break when paired with ô Fastô Deflection and ô Vigilanteô Vigor will set Shield Recharge Delay to 0. Due to Shield Gating, this makes shields effectively impossible to bypass except through Toxin or Magnetic status.

Media

Patch History

Hotfix 34.0.2 (2023-10-19)

Fixed Arcane Aegis exclusively triggering when not being attacked instead of when Shields are damaged. As reported here: https://forums.warframe.com/topic/1368186-arcane-aegis-massive-nerf
Update 31.6 (2022-06-09)

ARCANE STAT CHANGES PER RANK
Weãve adjusted the values of older Arcanes with the goal of making lower rank Arcanes more viable, and more desirable while collecting as a result. In short, there are three stats that each Arcane tends to have (speaking generally) -- for example, a percentage chance, a stat buff, and the duration of the buff.
Certain Arcanes increase all of these stats per rank (making them scale exponentially), but we are changing it so that only one stat is affected by rank (making it scale linearly). The other stats will now be at the max-rank value regardless of the Arcane Rank, thereby buffing lower-rank Arcanes as a result.

Arcane Aegis
On Shield Damaged: 3% (was 1%) chance for 5%/30% Shield Recharge for 12s (was 2s)
Update 17.9.1 (2015-10-29)

Introduced.

History
Arcane Agility was first introduced in the Trial mission The Law of Retribution, which was retired in Update 22.14 (2018-03-01).
The Operation: Scarlet Spear event sold Arcane Agility from Little Duck for 1,500 ô Scarletô Credits.
The Operation: Orphix Venom event sold Arcane Agility from Father for 300 ô Phasicô Cells.
The Operation: Gargoyle's Cry event sold Arcane Agility from the Vigile Jahu Gargoyle for 5 ô Grotesqueô Splinter.
The Operation: Belly of the Beast event sold Arcane Agility from Ordis for 3 ô Volatileô Motes in the first run of the event, and 4 Motes in the second, with a purchase limit of 42.
The Operation: Eight Claw event sold Arcane Agility from Dominus Thrax in bundles of 7, costing 2 ô Dominusô Aureus, with a purchase limit of 3 (21 Arcanes in total).
Patch History

Update 31.6 (2022-06-09)

ARCANE STAT CHANGES PER RANK
Weãve adjusted the values of older Arcanes with the goal of making lower rank Arcanes more viable, and more desirable while collecting as a result. In short, there are three stats that each Arcane tends to have (speaking generally) -- for example, a percentage chance, a stat buff, and the duration of the buff.
Certain Arcanes increase all of these stats per rank (making them scale exponentially), but we are changing it so that only one stat is affected by rank (making it scale linearly). The other stats will now be at the max-rank value regardless of the Arcane Rank, thereby buffing lower-rank Arcanes as a result.

Arcane Agility
On Damaged: 60% (was 10%) chance for 10%/60% Parkour Velocity for 18s (was 3s)
Update 22.12 (2018-02-09)

Now has a base value of 10% with a base chance of 3%.
Update 16.0 (2015-03-19)

Introduced.

Prior to Update 24.2 (2018-12-18), Arcane Arachne damage multiplier could be stacked upon subsequent wall latches, allowing for a large damage increase on the next hit.
Patch History

Update 31.6 (2022-06-09)

ARCANE STAT CHANGES PER RANK
Weãve adjusted the values of older Arcanes with the goal of making lower rank Arcanes more viable, and more desirable while collecting as a result. In short, there are three stats that each Arcane tends to have (speaking generally) -- for example, a percentage chance, a stat buff, and the duration of the buff.
Certain Arcanes increase all of these stats per rank (making them scale exponentially), but we are changing it so that only one stat is affected by rank (making it scale linearly). The other stats will now be at the max-rank value regardless of the Arcane Rank, thereby buffing lower-rank Arcanes as a result.

On Wall Latch: +25/150% damage for 30s (was 5s)
